title: neltuma alpataco f. rubra
intro: Neltuma alpataco f. rubra, a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, is commonly known as the Chilean bitter orange. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to act as a bitter and astringent, making it useful for treating digestive issues and promoting oral health. Neltuma alpataco f. rubra has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and sore throats, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
